insights.title: Paywall impressions per day by trigger (which gates fire)
Paywall click-through rate (unique users — view → purchase start)
Click-through rate by trigger (which gate actually sells)
Purchase starts per day by trigger
Immediate purchase completions per day by trigger (synchronous .completed only — Ask-to-Buy excluded, see App Store Connect for real units)
Start → immediate-completion rate (captures cancel/error drop-off in the sheet; pending Ask-to-Buy lands later via Transaction.updates and is NOT counted)
Purchase failure mix by reason (cancelled vs pending vs storeError)
Failure mix by trigger (gates with the worst drop-off)
Restore attempts per day — yielded Pro vs not (`restored` bool)

_exportMetadata.note: The IAP funnel — 'does it convert?' LIVE as of 1.1.0 (previously 'dark until 1.1 / RevenueCat'; rewritten v2, 2026-06-16). REWRITES FROM v1 (against the shipped AnalyticsEvent enum): event names corrected (`Paywall.Purchase.started` → `Pro.Purchase.started`, plus the NEW `Pro.Purchase.completed` / `.failed` / `.restored` cases that v1 didn't know about); the dead `Paywall.Screen.dismissed` insight removed (the event doesn't exist — the paywall sheet has only show + start); `product` parameter dropped (it's not sent — there's one SKU); `trigger` values aligned to the live PaywallTrigger enum (6 firing values + 2 reserved-unfired). SOURCE-OF-TRUTH SPLIT (now StoreKit 2, not RevenueCat — supersedes v1's plan-doc reference): App Store Connect owns real revenue, units sold, refunds, and the synced cross-device/Ask-to-Buy completion path. THESE TelemetryDeck insights are the behavioral funnel — which gate fires, which trigger drives the click, how the in-sheet outcome distributes. `Pro.Purchase.completed` here counts only synchronous `.completed` results inside the paywall — Ask-to-Buy / SCA approvals arrive later via `Transaction.updates` and are deliberately NOT re-emitted (would double-fire on cross-device or reinstall syncs; see AnalyticsEvent.swift line 199). So insight 5 is the immediate-completion rate, not the true conversion rate — read ASC for the latter. PAYWALL TRIGGERS (6 LIVE, 2 RESERVED): historyArchive · customParamHome · customParamSettings · settingsRow · advisorAccept · formatPicker — `export` and `sync` are declared in the enum for later surfaces and currently ship UNFIRED (insights here will not show those values until a gate raises one). The live tier=pro slice on insight 9/10 reads the EntitlementStore at signal-send time (flipped to `pro` the moment a purchase resolves; `tier` cannot be backfilled). Companion boards: Dashboard-LinkClean-Core-Usage.json (loop health — slice any insight by `tier` for the behavioral split), Dashboard-LinkClean-Growth-Surfaces.json (the 1.1 surface bets, including the `advisorAccept` and `formatPicker` trigger sources).

dynamicFields.name: Paywall.Screen.shown
dynamicFields.hint: Paywall presented, tagged with the gate that raised it. Carries `trigger`. The click-through denominator (insights 1/2/3).

dynamicFields.name: Pro.Purchase.started
dynamicFields.hint: Buy button tapped inside the paywall sheet. Carries `trigger`. The click-through numerator and the immediate-completion-rate denominator. (Renamed from the v1 `Paywall.Purchase.started`.)

dynamicFields.name: Pro.Purchase.completed
dynamicFields.hint: A purchase resolved synchronously inside the paywall sheet with `.completed`. Carries `trigger`. Ask-to-Buy / SCA approvals come back later via `Transaction.updates` and are NOT counted here — re-emitting would double-fire on cross-device/reinstall syncs (AnalyticsEvent.swift line 199). For real units sold, read App Store Connect.

dynamicFields.name: Pro.Purchase.failed
dynamicFields.hint: A purchase attempt produced no entitlement. Carries `reason` (cancelled / pending / storeError) and `trigger`. `pending` includes Ask-to-Buy / SCA-required cases that may complete later out-of-band — a high pending share alongside a low completion rate (insight 5) means the funnel is healthier than the synchronous rate suggests.

dynamicFields.name: Pro.Purchase.restored
dynamicFields.hint: A Restore Purchases attempt finished. Carries `restored` (bool) — whether it yielded Pro. The `restored=false` count is also the 'wrong button' read (users tapping Restore when they never bought).

dynamicFields.name: TelemetryDeck.Session.started
dynamicFields.hint: SDK-automatic session signal. Carries the `tier` default parameter, so unique users where `tier=pro` = paid actives (insights 9/10). Renamed from `newSessionBegan` in SDK 2.0 — verify the live type string.

dynamicFields.name: trigger
dynamicFields.hint: Which gate raised the paywall. Live values (PaywallTrigger enum, AnalyticsEvent.swift line 292): historyArchive (the 14-day history-archive lock) · customParamHome (locked add from the Home leftover pill) · customParamSettings (locked add from the Manage Parameters editor) · settingsRow (the Pro row on the Settings screen) · advisorAccept (1.1 — accepting an unknown-parameter advisor suggestion as a free user) · formatPicker (1.1 — choosing a Pro preset or saving a custom template in the Copy Formats editor). Reserved-unfired: `export`, `sync` (declared in the enum for later surfaces).

dynamicFields.name: reason
dynamicFields.hint: On Pro.Purchase.failed: cancelled (user closed StoreKit sheet) · pending (Ask-to-Buy / SCA — completion may arrive later via Transaction.updates) · storeError (StoreKit error).

dynamicFields.name: restored
dynamicFields.hint: On Pro.Purchase.restored: true (the attempt yielded Pro) / false (no prior purchase to restore).

dynamicFields.name: tier
dynamicFields.hint: Entitlement default parameter on EVERY signal (free / pro). Reads the EntitlementStore at signal-send time — flips to `pro` the moment a purchase resolves. Cannot be backfilled, so historical `tier=pro` reads only fill in from 1.1's release date forward.

dynamicFields.name: clientUser
dynamicFields.hint: TelemetryDeck's double-hashed anonymous user id (the App Group-shared *analytics* UUID — independent of the App Store Connect billing identity). Unique-user counts via thetaSketch.
